headline_finding: DENSE-COVERAGE DISCRIMINATION: 21 O&G wells fall within the plume-scale back-projection wedge (14 within 1-sigma). The nearest-centerline candidate is the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad (NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC, 14 co-located completions, ~0.6 km from S), but it CANNOT be isolated — the wedge is wide, the source point S is inherited from NASA's plume footprint (not self-derived), and the specific well is unresolved. No facility reaches HIGH. This is the dense-coverage analogue of Goturdepe's sparse-data finding, and gets the same first-class treatment.
scoring_disclaimer: Scores are a documented additive heuristic (weighted components shown), NOT calibrated probabilities. Confidence reflects DISCRIMINATION POWER, not proximity alone; rely on the qualitative tiers and the visible component rationales.
confidence_cap: No hypothesis exceeds MODERATE: dense but spatially-ambiguous coverage plus a NASA-anchored source localization cannot isolate a single facility. The nearest pad is MODERATE, not HIGH.

plume_summary.nearest_candidate: GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad (NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC)

global_assumptions: Steady ERA5 wind over the ~0.09 h plume transit.

WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (25.3 deg at 1-sigma, 43.4 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.69 m/s at |U10| 3.58 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. The low wind speed makes this wedge WIDE, which is the core reason facility-level isolation fails.

SEGMENTATION-DEPENDENCE: the source point S is derived from a NASA-anchored plume footprint (NASA L2B CH4PLM complex 000524), NOT a fully self-derived localization. Goturdepe's S came end-to-end from our own retrieval; this attribution inherits NASA's plume location. Treat the apex accordingly.
Plume-scale search radius 2 km (the source of a compact ~3.3 km plume sits at S +/- ~1 km, not tens of km upwind); wells beyond it are not candidates for THIS plume.

MODERATE-SOURCE REGIME: the retrieved rate (~0.85 t/hr [0.57-1.15]) is a moderate point source, consistent with a SINGLE well, small equipment, or a tank battery — not necessarily a large facility (Duren et al. 2019, doi:10.1038/s41586-019-1720-3; Cusworth et al. 2021, doi:10.1021/acs.estlett.1c00173). So facility TYPE barely discriminates among the well candidates; spatial proximity is the only real discriminator, and it is weak here.

HEADLINE (dense-coverage discrimination): 21 O&G wells fall within the plume-scale 2-sigma wedge (14 within 1-sigma). The nearest-centerline candidate (the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad, 14 co-located completions in the wedge) is FAVORED but NOT isolated — the data cannot pick the specific well, and cannot exclude the other 7 wells. No facility reaches HIGH; this is the dense-coverage analogue of Goturdepe's sparse finding.

candidate.descriptor: The GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad (operator NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC; nearest completion OGIM_ID 4311811) — nearest-centerline candidate
candidate.operator: NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC

hypotheses.claim: The ~0.85 t/hr plume's nearest-centerline candidate is the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad (NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC), ~0.6 km from the back-projected source S and ~0.4 deg off the wind azimuth. Pad/operator-level only: the pad holds 14 co-located completions and the specific well CANNOT be isolated, nor can the other 7 in-wedge wells be excluded.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.75 (band moderate): favored pad/operator-level candidate, but NOT HIGH — the evidence does not isolate a single facility.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: The GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad is the nearest-centerline candidate: 0.6 km from S, 0.4 deg off the upwind azimuth — closer in BOTH distance and angle than any other in-wedge well. But the wedge is wide (43 deg at 2-sigma), S is NASA-anchored, and the pad holds 14 co-located completions, so this is favored, NOT isolated — capped well below certainty.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: An active O&G well in a dense producing basin; a plausible moderate-source emitter. Type barely discriminates at this magnitude (see assumptions).

score_components.name: magnitude_consistency
score_components.rationale: ~0.85 t/hr is squarely in the moderate point-source regime — consistent with single-well venting/leak (Duren 2019; Cusworth 2021), neither too small nor super-emitter scale.

evidence.statement: The GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad's nearest completion (OGIM_ID 4311811, FAC_TYPE GAS, status COMPLETED) is 0.6 km from S at 0.4 deg off-centerline.

The GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad has 14 co-located completions inside the wedge (OGIM_IDs 4311811, 4313132, 4311790, 4312653, 4313174, 4312499..., operator NOVO OIL & GAS NORTHERN DELAWARE, LLC); a single overpass cannot resolve which one emits.
Emission rate 0.85 t/hr [0.57-1.15] (NASA-footprint-anchored) is a moderate single-source magnitude, well below super-emitter scale.
A VIIRS flaring detection (OGIM_ID 142757) lies 0.9 km from S within the wedge, corroborating active O&G in the area.
evidence.temporal_caveat: This detection is dated 2023-05-26, ~9 months AFTER the 2022-08-26 overpass — evidence of PERSISTENT activity, NOT about this plume, and NOT the located source. (Permian large emitters are intermittent: Cusworth et al. 2021, doi:10.1021/acs.estlett.1c00173.)

assumptions: Wind back-projection places the source upwind; S (NASA-footprint-anchored upwind tip) is the best source estimate.

WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (25.3 deg at 1-sigma, 43.4 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.69 m/s at |U10| 3.58 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. The low wind speed makes this wedge WIDE, which is the core reason facility-level isolation fails.

SEGMENTATION-DEPENDENCE: the source point S is derived from a NASA-anchored plume footprint (NASA L2B CH4PLM complex 000524), NOT a fully self-derived localization. Goturdepe's S came end-to-end from our own retrieval; this attribution inherits NASA's plume location. Treat the apex accordingly.
counter_considerations: 7 other O&G wells fall within the same wedge and cannot be excluded; proximity ranks the pad first but does not isolate it.
The specific emitting well on the pad is unresolved; even pad-level rests on a wide, speed-derived wedge and a NASA-anchored source point.
hypotheses.falsification: A facility-resolved overpass (or measured wind-direction variance tightening the wedge) placing the source on a different pad/well, or off this pad entirely, would falsify this.

candidate.descriptor: One of the 7 O&G wells in the wedge NOT on the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad

hypotheses.claim: The source may be any of the 7 in-wedge O&G wells that are NOT on the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad (and, within the pad, any of its 14 completions) — alternatives the wide, speed-derived wedge and NASA-anchored S cannot exclude. This non-discrimination IS the dense-coverage finding.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.51, band low: the indistinguishable-alternatives hypothesis — first-class, because dense coverage makes it real.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: Any of the 7 in-wedge wells NOT on the GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad could be the source; each is individually off-centerline or farther than the pad, but the wide wedge keeps them all non-excludable.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: Same O&G well prior as H1.

evidence.statement: 21 O&G wells fall within the plume-scale 2-sigma wedge (14 within 1-sigma). Rendered top 7 non-pad alternatives (cutoff at 8; all 21 are real OGIM records): LVP SWD #001 (OGIM_ID 4329880, 1.6 km, 13 deg); WELL #001 (OGIM_ID 4333328, 1.5 km, 13 deg); GOONCH FEDERAL COM 04 #215H (OGIM_ID 4311711, 0.8 km, 28 deg); SWEARINGEN #001 (OGIM_ID 4317735, 1.8 km, 30 deg); ARTEMIS FEDERAL COM #002 (OGIM_ID 4325465, 0.3 km, 36 deg); BCR FEDERAL #002 (OGIM_ID 4339400, 1.7 km, 40 deg); WELL #001 (OGIM_ID 4332555, 1.0 km, 40 deg).

assumptions: WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (25.3 deg at 1-sigma, 43.4 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.69 m/s at |U10| 3.58 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. The low wind speed makes this wedge WIDE, which is the core reason facility-level isolation fails.

SEGMENTATION-DEPENDENCE: the source point S is derived from a NASA-anchored plume footprint (NASA L2B CH4PLM complex 000524), NOT a fully self-derived localization. Goturdepe's S came end-to-end from our own retrieval; this attribution inherits NASA's plume location. Treat the apex accordingly.
counter_considerations: The GOONCH FEDERAL COM 0409 lease/pad is spatially favored (H1); these alternatives are ranked below it but not excluded.
hypotheses.falsification: Tighter localization (measured wind-direction variance, or a plume-resolved inversion) collapsing the candidate set to one pad would demote this.

candidate.descriptor: Non-O&G source (natural geologic seep or other sector)

hypotheses.claim: A non-O&G origin is possible but unlikely in a dense active O&G basin; stated for completeness, not because of positive evidence.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.36, band low: the active-basin context strongly disfavors a non-O&G origin but does not exclude it.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: Location does not discriminate sector; the discriminator against non-O&G is the type prior.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: A ~0.85 t/hr point source coincident with a dense active O&G basin is overwhelmingly O&G; a non-O&G origin (natural seep, other sector) is a very low prior but not excluded.

score_components.name: magnitude_consistency
score_components.rationale: A moderate point source is not characteristic of a natural seep here; magnitude disfavors it.

evidence.statement: The wedge lies in a dense active O&G basin (thousands of wells), which argues against a non-O&G origin; no in-situ measurement excludes a natural seep.

assumptions: Sector priors are heuristic, not site-measured; isotopic/in-situ confirmation would be needed to exclude a natural seep.
counter_considerations: No committed dataset evidences a natural seep here; included for honesty.
hypotheses.falsification: Isotopic (delta-13C) or in-situ sampling indicating non-thermogenic origin would resolve sector.

provenance.magnitude_prior_basis_2: Cusworth et al. 2021, ES&T Lett 8, doi:10.1021/acs.estlett.1c00173
